     The Numbers Business Report

Moonlight (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $27,854,931Details
International Box Office $36,973,516
Worldwide Box Office $64,828,447
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,384,078
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,263,228
Total $2,647,306
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A story of human connection and self-discovery, Moonlight chronicles the life of a young black man from childhood to adulthood as he struggles to find his place in the world while growing up in a rough neighborhood of Miami. The film is both a portrait of contemporary African American life and a personal meditation on identity, family, friendship, and love, portraying the moments, people, and unknowable forces that shape our lives and make us who we are.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$402,075 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:12.11 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:43.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$1,500,000 (worldwide box office is 43.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,564 max. theaters, 6.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,755,938

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 21st, 2016 (Limited) by A24
International Releases: January 20th, 2017 (Wide) (Australia)
January 20th, 2017 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 26th, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
January 27th, 2017 (Wide) (Mexico)
February 1st, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
Video Release: February 14th, 2017 by A24
MPA Rating: R for some sexuality, drug use, brief violence, and language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2443 (Cert #50681), 9/21/2016)
Running Time: 111 minutes
Award nomination: 2017 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Oscars Best Picture Winner, Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Autobiographical
Geographical setting: Miami
Plot point: African Americans, Closeted Homosexual, Coming of Age, LGBTQ+
Social setting: Hood Film
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Play
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: A24, Plan B Entertainment, Pastel
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

A story of human connection and self-discovery, Moonlight chronicles the life of a young black man from childhood to adulthood as he struggles to find his place in the world while growing up in a rough neighborhood of Miami. The film is both a portrait of contemporary African American life and a personal meditation on identity, family, friendship, and love, portraying the moments, people, and unknowable forces that shape our lives and make us who we are.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$402,075 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:12.11 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:43.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$1,500,000 (worldwide box office is 43.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,564 max. theaters, 6.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,755,938

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 21st, 2016 (Limited) by A24
International Releases: January 20th, 2017 (Wide) (Australia)
January 20th, 2017 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 26th, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
January 27th, 2017 (Wide) (Mexico)
February 1st, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
Video Release: February 14th, 2017 by A24
MPA Rating: R for some sexuality, drug use, brief violence, and language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2443 (Cert #50681), 9/21/2016)
Running Time: 111 minutes
Award nomination: 2017 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Oscars Best Picture Winner, Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Autobiographical
Geographical setting: Miami
Plot point: African Americans, Closeted Homosexual, Coming of Age, LGBTQ+
Social setting: Hood Film
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Play
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: A24, Plan B Entertainment, Pastel
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
